name: Vaikuntha Extended
description: Vaikuntha, the celestial abode of Lord Vishnu, represents the ultimate state of liberation and eternal bliss in Hindu cosmology. 'Vaikuntha Extended' delves into the deeper, more nuanced aspects of this sacred realm, exploring not only its physical description as a city of gold and jewels, but also its philosophical implications. It is a realm beyond the material universe, untouched by the cycles of birth and death, and free from suffering. The residents of Vaikuntha, the liberated souls known as the Muktas, exist in a state of perpetual devotion and service to Vishnu. The extended understanding of Vaikuntha encompasses the various levels and aspects of this divine realm, emphasizing the path of bhakti (devotion) as the means to attain it. It is a place of unparalleled beauty, filled with fragrant gardens, celestial rivers, and divine beings, all radiating the divine light of Vishnu. The significance lies not only in its physical description but in its representation of the ultimate goal of human existence – to transcend the limitations of the material world and attain eternal union with the divine. The 'Extended' aspect considers regional variations in descriptions and philosophical interpretations of Vaikuntha across different Hindu traditions.
subtitle: Abode of Vishnu, Realm of Bliss

keyMyths.title: The Promise of Vaikuntha
keyMyths.description: Lord Vishnu promises his devotees eternal residence in Vaikuntha upon attaining liberation. This promise motivates devotees to pursue the path of bhakti and righteousness.

keyMyths.title: Jaya and Vijaya's Curse
keyMyths.description: Jaya and Vijaya, the gatekeepers of Vaikuntha, were cursed to be born on Earth. This demonstrates the importance of humility and devotion, even in the highest realms.

sources.author: A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada
sources.description: A comprehensive translation and commentary on the Bhagavata Purana, detailing the nature of Vaikuntha.
sources.text: A comprehensive translation and commentary on the Bhagavata Purana, detailing the nature of Vaikuntha.

sources.author: Ramanujacharya
sources.description: A commentary on the Brahma Sutras, offering a Vishishtadvaita perspective on Vaikuntha.
sources.text: A commentary on the Brahma Sutras, offering a Vishishtadvaita perspective on Vaikuntha.
